questionText: Given that $\sin\theta = -\tfrac{8}{17}$ and that $\theta$ lies in the third quadrant ($180^\circ < \theta < 270^\circ$), find the exact values of $\sin 2\theta$, $\cos 2\theta$ and $\tan 2\theta$.

steps.description: Identify the signs from the quadrant
steps.explanation: In the third quadrant both sine and cosine are negative. The given $\sin\theta=-\tfrac{8}{17}$ is indeed negative, and this tells us $\cos\theta$ must come out negative too.

steps.description: Find cos theta from the Pythagorean identity
steps.explanation: Rearranging $\sin^2\theta+\cos^2\theta\equiv1$ gives $\cos\theta=\pm\sqrt{1-\sin^2\theta}$; the third-quadrant rule forces us to take the negative root.
This formula rewrites the double angle using only the single-angle ratios we now know.
The product of two negative ratios is positive, so $\sin 2\theta$ comes out positive.
The $1-2\sin^2\theta$ version is efficient here because $\sin\theta$ is already known, so no extra work is needed.
A single subtraction gives the exact cosine of the double angle.
Dividing the exact values already found reuses our work; the $289$s cancel cleanly.
As a safeguard the two squared values should add to $1$; they do, confirming the answers are consistent.
Both double-angle ratios are positive, which places $2\theta$ (measured in standard position) in the first quadrant and gives a quick sign check.
Collecting the three exact values completes the solution.
